title: zabelia biflora
intro: Zabelia biflora, also known as Korean boxwood or mountain sweet box, is a flowering plant species in the family Caprifoliaceae. It is a deciduous shrub native to Korea and Japan, known for its small white flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and branches of Zabelia biflora are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues.
